content.introduction: Heap sort builds a heap and repeatedly extracts the root to produce sorted order with guaranteed O(n log n) time.

sections.title: 7.7.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define heap property and relation between build-heap and repeated extraction steps.
examples: Explain max-heap use for ascending order
Contrast heap sort with quick sort behavior

sections.title: 7.7.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe in-place heap construction, root-end swap, and sift-down restoration process.
examples: Swap root with tail and shrink heap region
Sift down to restore heap property

sections.title: 7.7.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Complexity is O(n log n) time and O(1) extra space, but stability is not preserved.
examples: Compare heap sort memory advantage over merge sort
Use heap sort when worst-case guarantees are required

keyTakeaways: Guarantee O(n log n) worst-case runtime
